title: elymus canadensis f. calvescens
intro: Elymus canadensis f. calvescens, also known as Canadian wild rye, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae and is native to eastern North America. The medicinal properties of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Canadian wild rye include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and it is also used in herbal remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
